data.title: Israel AI czar lobbies TSMC, Intel, Samsung to build chip manufacturing hub: report
data.description:
Israel's head of the National Artificial Intelligence Directorate, Erez Askal, has recently lobbied chip manufacturers to develop a hub for producing semiconductors based on a 2nm or less process, according to Bloomberg.    

Askal, a former brigadier general in the Israeli military, has reached out to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Samsung (SSNLF) and Intel (INTC) as he works to build out the nation's chipmaking and AI infrastructure capabilities, the report said. He is also pushing for the development of a data center boasting 100,000 GPUs within the next five years. His efforts are complicated by Israel's ongoing war with Hamas stemming from the October 7, 2023, terrorist attack and a constrained electrical grid.

"I would view it as a very big failure if I don't get an advanced fab here from one of the companies," Askal said, according to the report.

Intel started to build its $25B "Fab 38" plant in Kiryat Gat, Israel, back in 2023. However, the project has since been paused due to changing market dynamics. Intel has about 9,000 employees based in Israel.

At the same time, some of Israel's neighbors are busy building out AI infrastructure, including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. 

"During the three years of war, no data centers in Israel were damaged, and they operated fully," Askal said in the report. "We see what's happening with our neighbors. We have a good relationship with them and are trying to help so that it won't happen there."

What's more, Israel's Electricity Authority announced on Monday it was suspending the processing of new data center grid connection requests for the next 140 days after applications reached 27 GW. To put that into perspective, Israel's all-time peak load reached 17 GW during a heat wave in 2025. It averages about 9 GW.

data.title: Bears take the wheel: Tesla stock breaches multi-month support after profit miss
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) shares plunged by double digits Thursday after the electric vehicle maker turned in second-quarter adjusted earnings below Wall Street expectations and posted its first quarterly cash burn in two years. Beyond the fundamental headlines, the selloff has inflicted significant technical damage on the stock's daily chart.

The steep intraday selloff of roughly -15% has dragged shares down to price levels last seen in September 2025. The sheer velocity of the decline is illustrated by the stock's 10-day rate of change, which has plummeted to roughly -21%—its most severe reading since April 2025.

The aggressive downward move forced TSLA to break out of a multi-month symmetrical triangle to the downside. The breakdown decisively rejected previous support zones, leaving the stock trading significantly below its closely watched 50-, 100-, and 200-day moving averages.

Other momentum indicators echo this bearish sentiment. The relative strength index, or RSI, is currently hovering just below 30, placing the stock slightly into oversold territory. This technical weakness is further compounded by a recent bearish moving-average-convergence-divergence crossover, suggesting that downward momentum is firmly in control as investors reassess the stock's near-term trajectory.

data.title: Pzena Investment Management adds CDW
data.description:
• Pzena Investment Management said it reduced positions in several financial and energy names during Q2 2026 while repositioning the portfolio toward new opportunities.
  • The firm initiated a new position in CDW (NASDAQ: CDW).
  • The firm did not disclose any complete portfolio exits.
  • Source.

data.title: Gentherm raises 2026 outlook while targeting $3.5B+ revenue by 2030 through Modine combination
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Gentherm (THRM) Q2 2026

Management view

  • "Strong commercial execution where we continue to significantly outperform the market, combined with operational discipline, resulted in an excellent first half, positioning Gentherm to deliver a solid year." (President, CEO & Director William Presley)

  • "Based on this performance, we are raising our full year 2026 guidance." (President, CEO & Director Presley) Presley also pointed to inflation management, saying the company is "proactively managing inflationary pressures through disciplined commercial actions and operational execution."

  • Beyond light vehicles, Presley said home-and-office traction is building: "In less than a year, we have successfully deployed our core technologies with 5 new customers and have visibility to $50 million to $100 million of revenue in this market by 2028." (President, CEO & Director Presley)

  • In medical, Presley announced regulatory progress and near-term commercialization: "we have received FDA 510(k) clearance for ThermAffyx" and "expect initial sales in the third quarter." (President, CEO & Director Presley)

  • On portfolio expansion, Presley said, "on July 1, we completed the strategic acquisition of Innovative Medical Equipment." (President, CEO & Director Presley)

  • On the Modine Performance Technologies transaction, Presley framed diversification and longer-term targets: "Our light vehicle mix will decrease from approximately 97% today to roughly 63%" and "The combined company will have a clear path to exceed $3.5 billion in revenue by 2030." (President, CEO & Director Presley)

  • "Revenue of $416 million was up 11% compared to the same period last year." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Jonathan Douyard)

Outlook

  • Douyard said Gentherm is "raising our 2026 full year guidance for revenue, adjusted EBITDA and adjusted free cash flow" and reiterated that 2026 guidance "excludes any impact related to our planned combination with Modine Performance Technologies." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • At the midpoint, Douyard said the company expects "revenue of $1.6 billion" and "adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $185 million to $200 million," adding that it implies "a midpoint margin of approximately 12%." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • Douyard maintained a quarterly margin cadence message: "we expect margins to remain lower in the third quarter before rebounding in Q4." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • On cash, Douyard guided "adjusted free cash flow between $85 million and $100 million" and "CapEx in the range of $45 million to $55 million." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • Versus the prior quarter, the company moved from maintaining guidance amid uncertainty to raising guidance; in Q1, Douyard said, "given the high level of uncertainty in the macro environment, we are maintaining our full year guidance at this time." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

Financial results

  • Douyard said Q2 revenue growth was helped by mix and volumes: "Revenues, excluding foreign currency translation, increased 9.5%, exceeding our expectations, driven by higher automotive volumes." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • On segment/product drivers, Douyard said Automotive Climate and Comfort Solutions revenue "increased 14.1% year-over-year" and that "Lumbar and Massage Comfort Solutions delivered another strong quarter of revenue growth at 38% year-over-year." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • Profitability commentary emphasized offsets: "We delivered $48.8 million of adjusted EBITDA or 11.7% of sales" and the company cited inflation recovery timing, inventory reductions tied to footprint actions, and "warranty accruals in both our automotive and medical businesses." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • EPS and merger-related costs were highlighted: "On a reported GAAP basis, diluted earnings per share were $0.14" and "Adjusted diluted earnings per share were $0.75," while GAAP EPS was "impacted by approximately $0.55 per share related to merger and restructuring expenses." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • Cash and balance sheet items included: "Adjusted free cash flow was approximately $16 million year-to-date"; "CapEx was $14 million"; "net leverage of 0.3 turns"; and "liquidity of $502 million." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

Q&A

  • Ryan Sigdahl, Craig-Hallum: Asked about concentration in auto awards and pipeline; President Presley said awards were "pretty well distributed" and added, "we remain confident in the second half. So it still looks like another robust year for awards." (President Presley)

  • Ryan Sigdahl, Craig-Hallum: Asked about IME purchase price and 2026 contribution; Executive VP Douyard said, "the purchase price is $34 million" and described IME as "a $17 million growth business today" with "20% EBITDA" and said it is "contemplated in what we put out from a guidance perspective." (Executive VP Douyard)

  • Ryan Sigdahl, Craig-Hallum: Asked about ThermAffyx demand signals and IME synergies; President Presley said, "Demand looks strong" and described channel complementarity: "IME serves over 200 Veterans Administration hospitals and clinics" while Gentherm has "0 access to the Veterans Administration today," concluding there is a "very, very strong cross-selling opportunity." (President Presley)

  • Nathan Jones, Stifel: Asked about sustainability of above-market auto growth; Executive VP Douyard said performance was "relatively broad-based," noting China strength, and added, "we've consistently said that this business can grow mid-single digit over market over time, and we're very confident." (Executive VP Douyard)

  • Nathan Jones, Stifel: Asked about home-and-office materiality and TAM; President Presley said wins with new North American brands were "more sizable," and reiterated, "the TAM in that market for us is over $500 million" while remaining "confident" in "$50 million to $100 million by 2028." (President Presley)

  • Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan: Asked about conservatism in the raise and 2027 update; Executive VP Douyard said, "we're not in a position to update" and added, "we feel highly confident that we'll be at the $1.7 billion or higher as we head into 2027," citing second-half headwinds from "runoff businesses." (Executive VP Douyard)

  • Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan: Asked about Modine commercial synergies; President Presley said, "we've become very much very confident in our ability to capture the $100 million plus that we talked about by 2030" and added, "over half of that will come from the valves business." (President Presley)

  • Glenn Chin, Seaport: Asked about warranty accrual drivers and persistence; President Presley said it was tied to "a very specific product with a specific customer" and concluded, "So we don't view this as something that continues in the run rate." (President Presley)

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts were slightly positive overall, pairing compliments with diligence on sustainability and integration details, including "is there some conservatism baked in" and whether warranty accruals should be "baking in going forward." (Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan; Glenn Chin, Seaport)

  • Management tone was positive and confident in prepared remarks and largely consistent in Q&A, using phrases such as "we are raising our full year 2026 guidance" and "we've become very much very confident" on cross-selling targets. (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard; President, CEO & Director Presley)

  • Compared with the prior quarter’s emphasis on uncertainty ("the macro and geopolitical environment has changed significantly"), the current call leaned more assertive on execution and capital deployment, including the new repurchase authorization. (President, CEO & Director Presley; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Guidance posture shifted from Q1’s "maintaining our full year guidance" to Q2’s "raising our 2026 full year guidance" on revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and adjusted free cash flow. (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • The Modine timeline became more specific in Q2, with Douyard saying, "we expect closing of the transaction to occur early in the fourth quarter" versus Q1’s expectation that it would "close later this year." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • Strategic diversification messaging remained consistent, but Q2 quantified post-combination mix more explicitly ("light vehicle mix will decrease... to roughly 63%") and reiterated the longer-range revenue framework. (President, CEO & Director Presley)

  • Analyst focus rotated from Q1’s inflation-cost recovery mechanics and organizational realignment to Q2’s sustainability of above-market growth, medical commercialization, and integration/cross-sell confidence. (Nathan Jones, Stifel; Ryan Sigdahl, Craig-Hallum; Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan)

Risks and concerns

  • Presley said the company continues to "monitor the macroeconomic and geopolitical environment" and is "proactively managing inflationary pressures" through commercial and operational actions. (President, CEO & Director Presley)

  • Douyard flagged near-term margin cadence risk, stating, "we expect margins to remain lower in the third quarter before rebounding in Q4." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Douyard)

  • Warranty accruals were a discrete issue in Q2; Presley said the company acted due to "increased claims" tied to a specific product and emphasized it is not viewed as ongoing run-rate pressure. (President, CEO & Director Presley)

Final takeaway

Management portrayed Q2 as a continuation of above-market automotive execution and operational discipline, alongside tangible steps to diversify: FDA clearance and planned Q3 initial sales for ThermAffyx, the completed $34 million IME acquisition with projected 2026 revenue of approximately $17 million and 20% EBITDA margins, and a more defined timeline to close Modine early in Q4. The company raised full-year 2026 guidance (excluding Modine) and paired that with a larger capital return signal via a new $400 million repurchase authorization, while maintaining that near-term margins are expected to be lower in Q3 before rebounding in Q4.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Dynagas allowed to continue transporting Russian LNG under new E.U. sanctions - FT
data.description:
Dynagas LNG (DLNG) up 5.4% in Thursday's trading following news that the European Union will exempt the company from new sanctions against Russia after pressure from the Greek government, according to the Financial Times.

The sanctions package, which ​is expected to be approved Thursday, would allow companies from the bloc ⁠to continue transporting Russia's LNG exports to third countries ​for a 12-month period that could be renewed, although volumes would ​be capped at 2025 levels.

Dynagas (DLNG) operates 27 gas carriers, including five of the 15 specialized Arc7 tankers that service the Yamal LNG plant, from which the E.U. has been buying record volumes of liquefied natural gas; the latest data, for this year's H1, showed that E.U. purchases of Yamal LNG rose by 16% Y/Y to an all-time high of 9.97M tons.

Greek shipowners control more than 20% of the global LNG carrier fleet, highlighting the country's significant role in LNG shipping.

data.title: Microsoft, Databricks expand partnership to scale enterprise AI
data.description:
Databricks and Microsoft (MSFT) have extended their strategic partnership into the 2030s to help scale enterprise AI.

Databricks will deepen its use of Microsoft's Azure, with Azure Databricks to run core business operations and build its unified lakehouse, while using Azure Cobalt, Microsoft’s next-generation Arm (ARM)-based infrastructure, to improve performance and efficiency.

Azure Databricks is a solution jointly developed by Databricks and Microsoft that unifies data, analytics, and AI on Microsoft's cloud computing platform Azure. 

The companies added that Microsoft will also continue integrating Databricks Data and AI platform across its products, bringing capabilities like Genie, Databricks' AI co-worker, directly into customer workflows.

"With Databricks Genie and Unity AI Gateway deeply integrated across Microsoft’s products, we're helping enterprises unify their data and ground AI in business knowledge. This lets customers get the full benefits of agents and models while controlling costs and ensuring governance," said Databricks Co‑founder and CEO Ali Ghodsi.

data.title: Altisource targets $45m run rate adjusted EBITDA by Q4 2028 as it signals flat Q3 and higher Q4 adjusted EBITDA
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Altisource Portfolio Solutions (ASPS) Q2 2026

Management View

  • "We are pleased with our second quarter performance with sequential and year-over-year service revenue growth in both segments," said William Shepro (Chairman & CEO), adding that "service revenue growth from customer wins has more than replaced the loss of a portion of the Rithm-related business" and that the company "reduced outstanding debt and continue to deploy AI and other efficiency initiatives."
  • Shepro tied margin plans to near-term cadence and a longer-term target: "We believe the continued ramp of sales wins and ongoing efficiency initiatives should drive roughly flat third quarter and higher fourth quarter adjusted EBITDA" and "achieve our Project 45 objective of $45 million in run rate adjusted EBITDA by the fourth quarter of 2028."
  • On diversification, Shepro said, "We are executing well against our plan to grow revenue and reduce our dependence on Onity and Rithm," and highlighted that "revenue from customers other than Onity Rithm and those associated with Onity and Rithm's portfolios increased to 65% of total service revenue from 46%."
  • Michelle Esterman (Chief Financial Officer) framed the company’s disclosures: "During this call, we will present both GAAP and non-GAAP financial measures" and "a reconciliation of GAAP to non-GAAP measures is included in the appendix to the quarterly slides."

Outlook

  • Shepro’s near-term earnings setup was explicit: "We believe the continued ramp of sales wins and ongoing efficiency initiatives should drive roughly flat third quarter and higher fourth quarter adjusted EBITDA."
  • On the durability of new business contribution in Servicer and Real Estate, Shepro said, "We anticipate service revenue from customer wins will continue to grow as it should take several more quarters for this new business to stabilize," and added, "We anticipate adjusted EBITDA to grow as service revenue from these wins continue to ramp."
  • Compared with Q1, management’s Q2 outlook language shifted from broad “full year service revenue growth” framing to a more specific quarterly adjusted EBITDA progression (flat Q3, higher Q4) and reiterated the multi-year Project 45 target.

Financial Results

  • Shepro reported, "For the second quarter, we generated service revenue of $48.7 million," and said it was "a 19% increase over the second quarter of 2025 and an 8% increase over last quarter."
  • On profitability and one-time items, Shepro said, "business segment and total company adjusted EBITDA and adjusted EBITDA margins declined quarter-over-quarter," citing "a non-recurring benefit realized in the second quarter 2025" and "higher costs to support revenue growth," while noting it was "partially offset by a second quarter 2026 gain from the repurchase of $2 million of our term loan."
  • On GAAP earnings, cash flow, and liquidity, Shepro said, "GAAP pretax earnings in the second quarter were nearly break-even," "net cash used in operating activities was $6.6 million," and "we ended the quarter with $23.2 million in unrestricted cash."
  • Segment detail included Servicer and Real Estate service revenue of $34.4 million and adjusted EBITDA of $11.7 million, which Shepro linked to "growth from customer wins" being "partially offset by a reduction of Rithm-related referrals" and "2026 Rithm-related EBITDA losses."

Q&A

  • Timothy D'Agostino, B. Riley Securities, Inc., Research Division: "could you maybe just provide some color on kind of how long it may take for inventory to transfer into revenue"; Chairman & CEO Shepro: "when you receive an REO file, it could take anywhere from... 9 to 12 months to sell" and "it takes around 12 months before it gets to the foreclosure sale."
  • D'Agostino, B. Riley Securities, Inc., Research Division: "the inventory wins in 1Q '26 still have runway to be realized"; Chairman & CEO Shepro: "Absolutely. Yes."
  • D'Agostino, B. Riley Securities, Inc., Research Division: "how do you think about capital allocation going forward"; Chairman & CEO Shepro: "we have the ability... to buy back up to $3 million in purchase price a year of debt" and "we want to continue to build the business, grow the revenue" while aiming "to ultimately refi the debt."
  • D'Agostino, B. Riley Securities, Inc., Research Division: "what percentage of service revenue Onity and Rithm might be going forward"; Chairman & CEO Shepro: "we do anticipate that... will decline over the next couple of months" and "maybe some continued decline in the third quarter... start to stabilize as we go into the fourth quarter."
  • Shachar Minkove, Napier Park Global Capital (US) LP: "Working capital seems to have been a bit of a use this quarter"; CFO Esterman: "I think it's associated with revenue growth" and "as revenues grow, you would expect receivables to grow a little bit as well," while Chairman & CEO Shepro added, "cash is already building back up this quarter."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was neutral-to-slightly negative in spots, pressing on conversion timing, liquidity, and working capital, including "Working capital seems to have been a bit of a use this quarter" (Minkove) and whether it was "something we should be too alarmed by" (Minkove).
  • Management sentiment was slightly positive, emphasizing momentum and control of concerns, including "We are pleased with our second quarter performance" (Chairman & CEO Shepro) and "No, I'm not alarmed at it" (CFO Esterman).
  • Versus Q1, management retained upbeat framing (“strong start” then; “pleased” now), but Q2 exchanges included more direct normalization language on Onity/Rithm runoff: "maybe some continued decline in the third quarter" and "start to stabilize" (Chairman & CEO Shepro).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q2 emphasized replacing Rithm-related losses with wins, stating "service revenue growth from customer wins has more than replaced the loss of a portion of the Rithm-related business" (Chairman & CEO Shepro), while Q1 emphasized being "off to a strong start" with improved GAAP pretax income versus the prior year.
  • Hubzu inventory remained a central KPI, moving from "17,200 assets as of March 31" in Q1 (Chairman & CEO Shepro) to "22,300 assets" in Q2 with "30%" quarter-over-quarter growth (Chairman & CEO Shepro).
  • Cash flow direction shifted from Q1 "Net cash provided by operating activities was $4.5 million" (Chairman & CEO Shepro) to Q2 "net cash used in operating activities was $6.6 million" driven by receivables (Chairman & CEO Shepro), prompting working-capital scrutiny in Q2 Q&A.

Risks and Concerns

  • Shepro flagged operating context: "We are performing well despite low delinquency rates and origination volumes" and said foreclosure metrics remain "significantly below pre-pandemic levels."
  • Customer concentration and transfer timing remained a risk variable, with Shepro stating revenue from certain Onity/Rithm portfolios "will decline over the next couple of months" and may see "some continued decline in the third quarter" before it "start[s] to stabilize" in Q4.
  • Working-capital usage was tied to growth, with Esterman stating receivables increased "associated with revenue growth," and Shepro characterizing it as "ordinary from our perspective."

Final Takeaway

Management described Q2 as a revenue-growth quarter driven by customer wins and Hubzu inventory expansion, while acknowledging quarter-over-quarter adjusted EBITDA margin pressure and a working-capital-driven operating cash outflow. The company’s near-term focus centered on efficiency initiatives (including AI deployment), opportunistic debt repurchases within covenant limits, and a Q3-to-Q4 adjusted EBITDA inflection, alongside a longer-term goal of reaching $45 million in run rate adjusted EBITDA by Q4 2028 and further reducing reliance on Onity and Rithm as portfolio transfers run off and stabilize.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nexstar slips as state AGs accuse the company of violating the Tegna integration order
data.description:
Nexstar Media (NXST) fell 2.7% after a group of state attorneys general and DirecTV accused the broadcaster of violating a court order that blocked the company from integrating with Tegna.

The state attorneys general claim that despite a preliminary injunction issued in April that prohibited the integration of the companies, Nexstar installed a Tegna board of directors, comprised of Nexstar's CEO, president, CFO, and general counsel, according to a court filing  on Wednesday.

"Nexstar cannot simultaneously control TEGNA’s Board and comply with an injunction requiring TEGNA to remain independent.," the states wrote in the court filing. "This should have been obvious."

In a statement to Bloomberg, Nexstar said Tegna remains independent and it “has scrupulously complied with the court’s hold-separate order. Nexstar has no involvement in Tegna’s retransmission consent negotiations, content decisions, staffing, or other day-to-day operations.” Nexstar executives on Tegna’s board are “critical to ensuring that Nexstar can continue to satisfy its financial reporting obligations while the hold-separate requirements are in place,” the company said.

A California judge on April 17 temporarily blocked the $6.2B deal from proceeding, which has been challenged ​by a dozen state attorneys general and DirecTV. The deal would create ​the largest broadcast station group in the United States, reaching ⁠80% of households.

A court hearing is set for Sept. 3 to discuss the preliminary injunction.

data.title: House narrowly passes $1.15 trillion defense bill as Senate hurdles remain
data.description:
The U.S. House of Representatives narrowly approved a $1.15 trillion defense authorization bill on Thursday, advancing annual Pentagon funding legislation that now faces an uncertain path in the Senate amid sharp partisan disagreements.

For investors, the bill signals continued bipartisan support for robust defense spending despite political divisions. If enacted, the measure would support military procurement, weapons development and defense contractors, although negotiations between the House and Senate are likely to reshape the final package.

The fiscal 2027 National Defense Authorization Act passed the House by a 216-212 vote, with most Republicans supporting the measure and most Democrats opposing it.

Republicans argued the legislation is needed to fund military operations, modernize defense capabilities and provide pay raises of up to 7% for service members, along with improvements to military housing.

Democrats criticized the bill's record spending level, particularly as Congress debates cuts to domestic programs. They also raised concerns about continued U.S. military involvement in Iran and a provision that would expand defense technology cooperation with Israel.

The annual defense authorization bill has been enacted for 65 consecutive years, but this year's version faces steeper political obstacles. The House attached separate voting legislation backed by President Donald Trump to the bill before sending it to the Senate, a move that could complicate its prospects because the voting measure lacks sufficient support in the upper chamber.

The Senate has yet to advance its own version after Democrats blocked consideration last week. Lawmakers must still pass separate House and Senate bills before negotiators can reconcile the differences and send a final version to the White House for the president's signature.

data.title: Gentherm soars after Q2 beat and raised outlook
data.description:
Gentherm (THRM) surged about 20.3% on Thursday after reporting second-quarter results that beat analyst expectations and raising its full-year guidance.

The thermal management company posted adjusted earnings per share of $0.75, topping the consensus estimate of $0.64, while revenue came in at $416.2 million, up 11% year over year and ahead of the $392.15 million estimate, marking a record quarter driven by growth in its automotive segment.

Net income rose to $4.4 million from $0.5 million a year earlier. However, operating income declined to $10.7 million from $24 million, while gross margin slipped to 23.2% from 23.9%, reflecting higher material costs and increased expenses.

Adjusted EBITDA rose to $48.8 million from $45.9 million, though margin edged lower to 11.7%.

“The Gentherm team demonstrated strong commercial performance with record quarterly revenue,” CEO Bill Presley said.

Gentherm (THRM) raised its 2026 outlook, now expecting revenue at a midpoint of about $1.6 billion. It also lifted adjusted EBITDA guidance to a midpoint of around $192.5 million.

The company also announced a new $400 million share repurchase program.

Shares have gained about 12.5% on a year-to-date basis, compared to a 9.5% rise in the broader index. Short interest on THRM stands at 5.09% of the total float.
